sanskrit: अधिष्ठानं तथा कर्ता करणं च पृथग्विधम् | विविधाश्च पृथक्चेष्टा दैवं चैवात्र पञ्चमम् ||
transliteration: adhiṣṭhānaṃ tathā kartā karaṇaṃ ca pṛthagvidham | vividhāś ca pṛthak ceṣṭā daivaṃ caivātra pañcamam ||
literal_meaning: The five factors for any action are: the physical locus, the agent, the instruments, the varied efforts, and the unseen higher-order factor (daiva).
contextual_meaning: Krishna reframes agency: instead of a single ego-doer, action emerges from five interacting layers. Owning only one’s intention, not the outcome, dissolves performance anxiety and moral paralysis.
psychological_state: From ego-centric responsibility to distributed, process-based participation—reduces burnout and moral fatigue.
trigger_scenario: A project manager blames herself for a product failure; realizing success depends on team, tools, market, effort, and timing, she shifts from self-flagellation to learning and next-step planning.
anchor_text: Do your part, leave the rest to life’s unseen weave.
